summary: Phase 14.2 Reactivate Lead workflow end-to-end testing. Backend pytest suite: 12/12 PASS covering RESTORE, CREATE, validation, 404, full RBAC matrix (SUPER_ADMIN/ADMIN/AGENT own + AGENT cross-403), and timeline CLIENT_REVERTED entries (CLIENT + ACTIVITY sources). Frontend Playwright: button visibility for SUPER_ADMIN, modal open, Shadcn Select interaction, Other-empty inline error, RESTORE path success banner + deep link to /leads/{id}, timeline updates with 2 reactivation entries, and AGENT viewing non-owned client cannot see the action group (page errors via /api/clients/:id 403, which is the correct stricter behavior). No regressions detected.
critical_code_review_comments: client-reactivation.service.ts correctly preserves linkedLeadId on RESTORE path (spec compliance verified by assertion).
CREATE path correctly snapshots fullName/phone/email/budget/preferredLocation/notes/assignedAgentId from client; lead.source='MANUAL', lead.status='NEW', tags=['reactivated'] — all verified.

Activity trail dual-write (ClientActivity + lead Activity with action='CLIENT_REVERTED') confirmed on the unified timeline endpoint — both CLIENT and ACTIVITY source entries surfaced with the reason in description/metadata.
RBAC gating in controller (isAdminLevel || assignedAgentId === user.id) verified against all three roles + a freshly-provisioned cross-agent.
Validation (missing reason -> 400 'Reason is required'; >500 chars -> 400) verified.

Minor observation (non-blocking): AGENT viewing a client they don't own currently 403s on GET /api/clients/:id (existing behavior), so the ClientDetailPage falls into the error state and the reactivate-lead-button is not rendered. The button visibility is hence guarded both by RBAC fetch-failure AND the canManage gate, which is acceptable / stricter than the spec wording 'hidden to AGENT viewing a client NOT assigned to them'.
test_credentials: Used credentials from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (SUPER_ADMIN admin@realestate.com, ADMIN manager@realestate.com, AGENT agent@realestate.com).
seed_data_creation: Created (and deleted in TestZZCleanup): ~10 TEST_-prefixed clients, ~7 TEST_-prefixed leads, 1 TEST_agent_{ts}@realestate.com AGENT user (disabled at teardown). All idempotent via timestamp + uuid suffixes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reactivation regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_client_reactivation.py — run with `pytest backend/tests/test_client_reactivation.py -v` from /app. ~72s wall (real Neon DB, no mocks). Cleanup is self-contained. Frontend deep-links use href=/leads/{leadId}; Radix Select option testids follow pattern reactivate-reason-option-{slug} (e.g. -other, -budget-issue). For AGENT 403 visibility, the page currently shows client-detail-error state because /api/clients/:id itself 403s for AGENTs on unowned clients — this is existing controller behavior in getOneClient, not something introduced by Phase 14.2.
